visual_features.overall_style: Soft, modern, and minimalist marketing site with a warm, welcoming feel. Uses a pastel cream background with a deep desaturated blue for typography, plus soft blue pills for CTAs. Overall flat design with very subtle depth from borders rather than heavy shadows. The look is clean and calm, matching a premium home renovation brand.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven by size, weight, and color contrast. The logo and primary navigation sit on a single horizontal line at the top. The main H1 (“Renovation and Extension Builder Newcastle”) is the strongest focal point: centered, large, and bold in the darkest blue. Below it is multi-line body copy in smaller size and regular weight, still centered. A single prominent CTA button sits below the copy, isolated with whitespace to stand out. At the bottom edge of the hero, a row of project images forms a secondary focal band that hints at the portfolio and visually anchors the hero.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Generous vertical spacing in the hero: large top padding between nav and heading, large gap between heading, paragraph text, and the primary CTA. The left and right margins are wide, with content constrained to a centered column. The navigation links have ample horizontal spacing, and the contact button has extra padding and roundness to give it breathing room. The row of images below has narrow gutters but still visible separation, ensuring it doesn't feel cramped.
unique_elements: Pill-shaped navigation 'Contact' button that visually differentiates it from the text links.
Centered hero with warm cream full-bleed background that creates a soft, editorial feel rather than a typical hero image banner.
Outline, pill-shaped primary CTA ('See our projects') that uses the brand blue for border and text, visually linking it with the nav links.
Continuous cream background from header through hero, visually separated from the image strip by a sharp horizontal edge.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit centered column layout likely based on a 12-column grid with a max-width content container (approx. 1040–1200px). The image row at the bottom suggests a 3–4 column grid with equal-width columns.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Full-width header and hero with centered content, followed by a full-width image band. Navigation is horizontal and right-aligned; hero content is vertically stacked and center-aligned.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top section: header with logo on the left and primary navigation on the right on a single horizontal row. Below that, the hero section is structured as: heading, supporting paragraph text, primary CTA button, then a hard edge into a portfolio image strip. Sections use clear vertical separation with generous padding rather than visible dividers.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Navigation items are right-aligned within the header; the logo is left-aligned. All hero text and the primary CTA are center-aligned horizontally. The image thumbnails below appear left-aligned within their grid container but overall centered within the page frame. Vertical alignment is strongly stacked, reinforcing a clear top-to-bottom scan.
responsive_hints: Centered single-column hero text and CTA suggest an easy collapse to mobile (logo on top, burger or stacked nav below, then hero text and CTA).
Image strip likely collapses from multiple columns to a 2-column or single-column grid on smaller screens.
Pill-shaped buttons and ample padding indicate touch-friendly tap targets on mobile.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Content flows in a linear top-to-bottom sequence: (1) Header row with logo left and nav right. (2) Hero block with a single centered headline line, followed by a multi-line descriptive paragraph, followed by a centered primary CTA button. (3) Immediately underneath, a full-width strip of project images arranged in a horizontal grid (likely 3–4 equal-width image tiles). The hero content is constrained to a central column and does not span the full browser width; the image grid appears to span the usable content width with equal gutters.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Within the viewport, approximately the top 15–20% is used by the header and top margin, ~50–55% by the hero (headline, copy, CTA) with abundant vertical whitespace around text, and the remaining portion by the horizontal image strip at the bottom. Horizontal margins around the hero text are generous, effectively creating a wide central column with roughly 15–20% whitespace on each side on desktop. The CTA has extra padding and margin around it, making it occupy a visually distinct island in the center.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Spacious, editorial-style layout. Text lines are comfortably spaced with generous line-height and margins between blocks (heading → paragraph → CTA). Elements are not crowded; there is clear breathing room around the navigation items and logo, and the hero occupies relatively few elements for the space it uses. The image strip is denser than the hero text but still balanced by consistent gutters and margins.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye naturally follows a simple Z-to-vertical pattern: first left to right across the top (logo → About → Portfolio → Contact button), then down to the large center heading. From the heading, the eye moves down through the paragraph text and then to the centrally located CTA button. The bottom row of images then pulls the gaze horizontally left-to-right, previewing portfolio content and inviting scrolling to see more. Visual flow is reinforced by centered alignment and a single brand color guiding from nav text to heading to CTA.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons are pill-shaped rounded rectangles (approx. 24–28px corner radius). The primary hero CTA ('See our projects') is an outline button: transparent fill, 1–2px border in the brand navy (#244A7C), and navy text. It has generous horizontal padding (roughly 20–28px left and right) and moderate vertical padding (10–14px), giving a compact yet prominent pill appearance. Text is sentence case, medium or semibold weight. The 'Contact' button in the navigation is a filled pill with a soft light-blue background (#C8E2FF) and navy text, with no visible border. Both appear flat (no shadow, no gradient), consistent with a modern flat design.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Cards are implied by the portfolio image strip at the bottom. Each 'card' is essentially an image tile with a clean edge. Corners appear very slightly rounded or square (0–4px radius). There is no visible heavy shadow; separation is provided by white or light-neutral gutters between images. Any further card content (titles, text) likely sits beneath the image or overlays with minimal styling. The emphasis is on the photography, not heavy framing.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Forms are not visible in the screenshot, but based on the overall style we can infer: inputs would likely be simple rectangles with slightly rounded corners (4–8px radius), light neutral background or white, and a 1px border in a soft grey (#CCCCCC) or blue on focus. Labels would likely sit above the inputs, left-aligned, with small to medium body text, and sufficient vertical spacing between fields to maintain the airy feel. The primary submit button would follow the pill-style CTA pattern, either filled blue or outlined navy on cream background.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected layout: stacked labels with full-width inputs within a form container, e.g., `<form><label>Label</label><input ... /><label>Label 2</label><input ... /></form>`. Grouping for related fields could use `<fieldset>` with subtle top spacing rather than heavy visual dividers. Validation feedback would use small helper text below inputs, styled in a semantic color (e.g., red for errors).

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary navigation is a horizontal top bar on a cream background. Logo at far left, nav links (About, Portfolio) aligned right as text-only links, and a visually distinct 'Contact' pill button at the far right. Nav links are likely simple text with no underline, using the navy color. Spacing between links is generous (20–32px). Active or hovered states are probably indicated by color changes (slightly darker text or underline) and for the Contact button, a slight tint change or darker blue. The bar has no visible border or shadow; it blends seamlessly with the hero background.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit textual list is visible, but the image strip acts like a visual list of projects: low-density items with significant width, equal spacing, and no visible bullet or numbering. In future sections, service or feature lists would likely follow a similar style: spaced-out items, perhaps with small icons or thumbnails on top, and text underneath, maintaining ample vertical padding.

heading.visual_style: Rounded, modern geometric sans-serif with a high x-height and smooth curves. The letters are clean and slightly wide, giving a contemporary, approachable tone appropriate for a lifestyle/renovation brand.

body.visual_style: Clean humanist/neo-grotesque sans-serif with good readability and moderate stroke contrast, suitable for multi-line paragraphs.

font_families.code: Source Code Pro, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, 'Courier New', monospace

spacing_system.component_padding: Buttons and pills use approximately 8px vertical and 20–24px horizontal padding (2–3 base units). Text blocks have ~16–24px spacing above and below. Image cards have 8–16px gaps between them.
spacing_system.section_margin: Major sections (header to hero content, hero to image strip, etc.) use roughly 48–72px vertical spacing (6–9 base units), producing a spacious rhythm between sections.

interaction_hints: Buttons likely have hover states with background color fill for outline buttons (cream to light-blue or navy fill with inverted text) and a slightly darker shade for filled pills. Border color might intensify on hover.
Navigation links likely change color or gain an underline on hover; the Contact pill may slightly darken or show a subtle inner shadow to indicate interactivity.
Transitions should be smooth and subtle (150–250ms ease-in-out) for color and background changes to match the calm, premium brand feel.
Image tiles in the portfolio strip might use a slight scale-up or shadow on hover to suggest clickability, with a gentle transition to avoid feeling abrupt.
CTA buttons may feature a slight focus ring or outline on keyboard focus to maintain accessibility while matching the rounded pill style.
